growth_records.medium: Kim medium (with FeSO4·7H2O; CO2 as carbon source)

up.compound: ferrous iron (Fe2+, from FeSO4·7H2O 44 g/L; energy substrate)

growth_records.cite: Saavedra A et al. Biooxidation of Iron by Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans in the Presence of D-Galactose: Understanding Its Influence on the Production of EPS and Cell Tolerance to High Concentrations of Iron. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.00759. [PMC7191041]
growth_records.snippet: Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans in the absence of D-galactose exhibited a specific maximum growth rate (μmax) of 0.11 h–1 in Kim medium ... reached a cell concentration of approximately 2.2 × 10^8 cells mL–1
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.11 h-1 measured (reported, Fig 1A). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.11 = 6.30 h. Chemolithoautotroph: energy from Fe2+ oxidation, carbon from CO2. Numeric specific/volumetric Fe3+ productivity (qFe3+, QFe3+) reported only in figures (Fig 5), no text values, so uptake/secretion rate fields null but flagged is_experimental:true (Fe2+ consumed, Fe3+ produced, measured by phenanthroline). Reached ~2.2e8 cells/mL.

growth_records.cite: Saavedra A et al. Biooxidation of Iron by Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans in the Presence of D-Galactose: Understanding Its Influence on the Production of EPS and Cell Tolerance to High Concentrations of Iron. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.00759. [PMC7191041]
growth_records.snippet: in the presence of D-galactose, it showed a lower μmax of 0.009 h–1 ... those microorganisms subjected to D-galactose, barely reached 0.5 × 10^8 cells mL–1
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.009 h-1 measured (reported, Fig 1A) under 0.25% D-galactose. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.009 = 77.0 h. Growth strongly inhibited by D-galactose in unadapted cells at low density; reached only ~0.5e8 cells/mL. Fe2+/Fe3+ specific rates in figures only (null).

growth_records.medium: Kim medium (chemostat; D-galactose inducer fed at 0, 0.15, 0.25, 0.35% w/v across steady states)
growth_records.cite: Saavedra A et al. Biooxidation of Iron by Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans in the Presence of D-Galactose: Understanding Its Influence on the Production of EPS and Cell Tolerance to High Concentrations of Iron. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.00759. [PMC7191041]
growth_records.snippet: the discontinuous mode was changed to continuous at a dilution rate (D) of 0.03 h−1 ... Chemostat operated at D = 0.03 h−1, pH 1.8, and 30°C. Samples were taken at steady state conditions.
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat: mu = D = 0.03 h-1 (given). doubling_time = ln2/D = 0.6931/0.03 = 23.10 h. Same D across four steady states differing in D-galactose feed (0/0.15/0.25/0.35% w/v); iron biooxidation similar across states, bacterial density constant at 0.15-0.25% but decreased at 0.35%. Fe2+/Fe3+ specific rates (Fig 3) figure-only, null.
